---
title: "Auto-generated briefs"
subtitle: "Two more engagement types, straight out of describe() — unedited"
---

The [case description](case-description.qmd) is a hand-curated case: a
real `grocery-sim` run, but with a full [stakeholder report](stakeholder-report.qmd)
written on top of it, worked and checked line by line. The two briefs below
are different on purpose — they are the **raw, unedited output of
`sim.describe()`**, shown to demonstrate that the brief generator itself
produces genuinely different kinds of engagements depending on what
actually happened in the run, not just different flavor text on the same
story.

`grocery-sim` classifies every run along two axes: how the business is
actually doing (`struggling` / `thriving` / `uncertain`, from the real
profit trajectory) and what kind of engagement that performance calls for
(`diagnose` a specific cause, `optimize` day-to-day operations, or `invest`
retained earnings — see [Theory](../theory.qmd) for the reasoning behind the
split). Malm's Market is a `diagnose` case. The two below are `optimize` and
`invest`, generated from `grocery_sim.recipes` — named, empirically
verified settings recipes that reliably land on a given engagement type
(shipped in the package specifically so a case-writer doesn't have to hope a
random seed produces the exercise they want).

Neither brief below has an accompanying analysis report — per `describe()`'s
own closing line, each is "a starting outline, not a finished case."

## Optimize: an operationally sloppy year

Generated from `grocery_sim.recipes.RECIPES["optimize"]` — a struggling
one-year run with elevated, cost-priced shrinkage. The engagement reads as
"find where I'm bleeding money," not "find the one thing that went wrong."

{{< include _generated/optimize_brief.md >}}

## Invest: real, unspent retained earnings

Generated from `grocery_sim.recipes.RECIPES["invest"]` — a thriving
three-year run where retained earnings have cleared the smallest
investment's own calibrated capex. The engagement reads as a capital
question, not a diagnosis: what should the owner do with the money.

{{< include _generated/invest_brief.md >}}
